hero.hero_title_1:
Run Any Database Natively on
Amazon EKS -
Without the Compromise.
hero.hero_description: Deploy and manage 25+ Production-grade databases as native Kubernetes resources on Amazon EKS. Leverage AWS infrastructure with broader choice, zero extra licensing, and true GitOps agility.

paragraph.content: KubeDB by AppsCode delivers a production-grade, Kubernetes-native Database-as-a-Service platform that runs seamlessly on Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS). It enables platform teams and developers to provision, manage, scale, backup, and recover a wide range of databases using familiar Kubernetes APIs and declarative workflows - without vendor lock-in or reliance on hyperscaler-managed database services.

what_this_means.heading: What This Means for AWS and EKS Users
what_this_means.description: If you're running applications on Amazon EKS, you already enjoy scalable, managed Kubernetes. However, databases often introduce complexity through separate tooling, manual operations, or cloud-specific DBaaS services that create lock-in.
what_this_means.subtitle: KubeDB changes that by making databases first-class Kubernetes resources directly inside your EKS clusters:

items.title: Faster Time-to-Value
items.description: Launch databases in minutes instead of weeks.

items.title: Lower Operational Burden
items.description: Automate Day-2 tasks and eliminate custom scripts.

items.title: Cost Optimization
items.description: Use existing AWS storage and avoid premium managed DBaaS pricing.

items.title: Hybrid/Multi-Cloud Consistency
items.description: Same workflows whether running on EKS or other clouds.

architecture.heading: KubeDB runs natively on Amazon EKS
architecture.description: The KubeDB Operator sits directly on top of your EKS clusters, leveraging AWS infrastructure, multi-AZ capabilities, and Kubernetes-native patterns for database automation.

layers.label: KubeDB Platform
layers.desc: Web Console • Envoy Gateway • Hub/Spoke Model • Policy Engine

layers.label: Kubernetes Services
layers.desc: ArgoCD • Open Cluster Management • Cert Manager • Prometheus • Grafana

architecture.image_alt: KubeDB Platform Architecture on Amazon EKS

capabilities.heading: Production databases. Fully automated on EKS.
capabilities.description: 25+ Database engines with HA, scaling, backup, TLS, monitoring, and GitOps support

items.title: Run Databases Natively on EKS
items.description: KubeDB integrates deeply with EKS, supporting any Kubernetes StorageClass (including EBS, EFS, or third-party CSI drivers). Databases run inside your private pod network with full affinity and anti-affinity controls for Multi-AZ resiliency.

items.title: DBaaS Without Cloud Lock-In
items.description: Complete data ownership and sovereignty with portability across clouds or on-prem. Support for air-gapped and sovereign environments with your choice of storage optimized for performance, cost, or resilience.

items.title: Broad Database Support
items.description: Manage 25+ Database engines including PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, Kafka, and more, all with consistent Kubernetes-native workflows.

items.title: Automated Lifecycle Management
items.description: Declarative CRDs handle provisioning, upgrades, scaling and repair with zero-downtime where possible.

items.title: High Availability & Resiliency
items.description: Multi-AZ clustering, automated failover, PodDisruptionBudgets, and continuous health checks.

items.title: Backup & Recovery
items.description: Integrated with KubeStash for policy-driven, incremental, encrypted backups to S3. Support for point-in-time recovery.

items.title: Monitoring & Observability
items.description: Native Prometheus + Grafana dashboards. Integrates with Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus.

items.title: Security & Governance
items.description: Automated TLS, Transparent Data Encryption, RBAC integration with EKS IAM, network policies, and Vault support.

items.title: Self-Service for Developers
items.description: Platform teams enforce policies while developers provision databases via Kubernetes-native workflows.

database_support.heading: Comprehensive Database Support
database_support.description: 25+ Database engines managed with Kubernetes-native workflows

categories.label: Relational
categories.items: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, Percona XtraDB

aws_integration.heading: Seamless AWS & Kubernetes Integration
aws_integration.description: KubeDB leverages the full power of AWS and Kubernetes to deliver a complete database platform

integrations.title: Amazon EKS & Kubernetes
integrations.description: Deploy databases natively on Amazon EKS, Red Hat OpenShift, Rancher, or any Kubernetes distribution. Full support for Kubernetes operators, Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs), and GitOps workflows.

integrations.title: Amazon S3
integrations.description: Automated backup and archival to S3 with lifecycle policies, versioning, cross-region replication, and support for GCS, Azure Blob, and other object stores.

integrations.title: Amazon CloudWatch & Prometheus
integrations.description: Deep integration with CloudWatch and native Prometheus support. Pre-built Grafana dashboards, custom alerts, and automated incident response.

integrations.title: AWS IAM & Kubernetes RBAC
integrations.description: Use AWS IAM for authentication and support for Kubernetes RBAC for fine-grained access control. Support for IAM roles, service accounts, and HashiCorp Vault for credential management.

integrations.title: AWS VPC & Network Policies
integrations.description: Deploy databases in your VPC with custom network configurations, security groups, and private subnets. Full support for Kubernetes NetworkPolicies.

items.title: AWS KMS
items.description: Encryption key management & TDE

items.title: EBS Volumes
items.description: High-performance block storage

items.title: Route 53
items.description: DNS management

items.title: AWS Secrets Manager
items.description: Credential rotation

items.title: CloudFormation
items.description: Infrastructure as code

items.title: AWS Certificate Manager
items.description: TLS/SSL certificates

items.title: Auto Scaling
items.description: Dynamic scaling policies

items.title: Datadog & New Relic
items.description: Third-party monitoring

cost_comparison.heading: Why KubeDB Is More Cost-Effective Than AWS RDS
cost_comparison.description: Run databases on AWS at a fraction of the cost while improving performance and developer productivity

savings_cards.title: Better Bin Packing
savings_cards.description: Run multiple databases on the same EC2 host, unlike RDS which requires dedicated instances

savings_cards.title: Spot Instance Support
savings_cards.description: Use AWS Spot Instances with Karpenter for database pods, reducing compute costs by up to 90%

savings_cards.title: Lower Management Fees
savings_cards.description: RDS charges premium management fees on top of EC2 costs. KubeDB fees are significantly lower

savings_cards.title: Free for Short-Lived Workloads
savings_cards.description: Use KubeDB free for any clusters running less than 30 days (dev, test, CI/CD environments)

benefit_panels.title: Developer Productivity Benefits
benefits: Every developer gets their own database instance
Preview environments for every pull request
Provision databases in under 60 seconds vs 5-10 minutes
Use same tools (Helm, Prometheus) for apps and databases

benefit_panels.title: Operational Advantages
benefits: No vendor lock-in - reuse DevOps workflows on-prem or any cloud
Lower latency with databases in same cluster as apps
Support for additional databases like Elasticsearch, Kafka, etc

savings_cta.heading: Calculate Your Savings
savings_cta.description: Organizations typically save 60-70% on database costs when switching from RDS to KubeDB, while improving provisioning speed and developer productivity

industries.heading: Trusted Across Industries
industries.description: Organizations rely on KubeDB for mission-critical databases across diverse industries and use cases

items.title: Financial Services & Healthcare
items.description: Meet strict compliance requirements with data sovereignty, air-gapped deployments, and full control over data locality. SOC 2 and HIPAA compliant infrastructure.

items.title: Multi-Tenant SaaS Applications
items.description: Deliver isolated database instances for each tenant with automated provisioning, lifecycle management, and policy-driven governance at scale.

items.title: Hybrid & Multi-Cloud Deployments
items.description: Run databases consistently across AWS, on-premises, and other clouds. Avoid vendor lock-in with portable Kubernetes-native infrastructure.

items.title: Edge & IoT Workloads
items.description: Deploy databases at the edge with automated backups to central storage. Support for disconnected operations and data synchronization.

testimonial.quote: InterSystems was delighted to engage with KubeDB in the delicate, yet fundamental task of supporting durable, non-ephemeral workloads with Kubernetes. ... I would recommend KubeDB consulting for any Kubernetes related work.
testimonial.author: Luca Ravazzolo

getting_started.heading: Getting Started on AWS EKS
getting_started.description: Deploy KubeDB on your EKS cluster in minutes

steps.title: Deploy KubeDB
steps.description: Via Helm or Operator Lifecycle Manager on your EKS cluster

steps.title: Create Database
steps.description: Define a database Custom Resource with desired spec

steps.title: Configure Backups
steps.description: Set up backups to Amazon S3 and enable monitoring

steps.title: Manage Operations
steps.description: Scale, upgrade, or failover using Kubernetes tools

cta.heading: Run Databases Natively
cta.description: 60% cost savings, 10x faster provisioning, zero vendor lock-in
